comments.text: Carthaginian engineering

comments.author: @TheTruth-mr3ml
comments.text: Carthaginian engineering

replies.author: @trueglobe66
replies.text:
Yes, Carthaginian engineering was truly fascinating. Their influence in North Africa and across the Mediterranean is still one of the most interesting parts of ancient history.
What part of Carthaginian history interests you most: the architecture, the naval power, Hannibal, or the conflict with Rome?

comments.author: @TheTruth-mr3ml
comments.text: Science has advanced and debunked the Phoenician myth, proving that the Carthaginians have no connection to the so-called Phoenicians because they do not possess Levantine genes. I kindly advise you to review the genetic studies published in recent years and not to perpetuate the Dido myth.

comments.text: Bellissime immagini della meravigliosa Tunisia, ma descrizione confusa, due esempi all'inizio quando descrivi la Medina di Tunisi la maggior parte delle immagini immortalano la Medina di sousse,  descrivi Touzer ma le immagini si riferiscono alle oasi di montagna, questa narrazione che non corrisponde alle immagini é un vero peccato, il video è bellissimo

comments.author: @OpenEarth-4k-travel-channel
comments.text: From Carthage to underground homes in Matmata , this isn’t a country, it’s layers of civilizations stacked on top of each other. Honestly, one of the most underrated places in the world.

replies.author: @trueglobe66
replies.text: Exactly, that’s what makes Tunisia so special. It feels less like one country and more like several worlds somehow living in the same place.

comments.author: @mikesweeden
comments.text: Thanks for the very interesting history.🤗

replies.author: @trueglobe66
replies.text: Tunisia really has the kind of history that keeps giving and giving 😄

comments.author: @WorldDiscovered_Official
comments.text: I was lucky enough to visit Tunisia once, and honestly, this country is far more beautiful and mysterious than what people usually see in the news. Beyond the beaches and tourist resorts, there are ancient ruins, desert villages, old stone streets, and a deep sense of history everywhere you go. What impressed me most was how Tunisia feels both Mediterranean and desert at the same time — warm, ancient, quiet, and full of stories. This documentary captures a side of Tunisia that many people never get to see.

comments.text: Tunisia is really such a beautiful place. Amazing❤
